highlights: Easternmost point on Vietnam's mainland where sunrise first greets the country daily
Historic lighthouse built by French in 1890 atop 110m peak with panoramic ocean views
Scenic coastal road with mountains on one side and blue sea on the other

thingsToKnow.difficulty: Hiking 1-1.5km uphill to lighthouse with low steps; alternatively motorbike taxis available but officially prohibited from carrying tourists
thingsToKnow.safety: Unofficial motorbike taxis operate illegally without helmets or proper permits; use official transportation or hike instead
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Motorbike taxis available for ~100,000 VND per 2 people one-way; alternative is 15-minute walk then stair descent
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Best visited at sunrise; afternoon for sunset also recommended; weather variable, check conditions before visiting

visitorTips: Wear comfortable athletic shoes or low-heeled sandals for easy walking on mountain trails
Arrive very early to witness first sunrise from mainland Vietnam at the lighthouse
Explore Mon Beach below with pristine white sand and clear water after visiting lighthouse

faq.question: What is the entrance fee?
faq.answer: Entrance fee is 30,000 VND per adult; parking is 10,000-20,000 VND. Purchase ticket at gate or pay upon exit if gate staff unavailable.

faq.question: How do I reach the lighthouse?
faq.answer: Hike 1-1.5km uphill (15 minutes) with low steps, or use motorbike taxi for 100,000 VND one-way. Hiking recommended to avoid unofficial taxis.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit?
faq.answer: Early morning to witness first sunrise from mainland Vietnam. Afternoon sunset also beautiful. Avoid midday heat. Located 35km from Tuy Hoa city.

sentiment.positive: Visitors consistently praise stunning natural scenery, dramatic coastal views, historic lighthouse, and significance as first sunrise point on Vietnam's mainland
sentiment.negative: Unofficial motorbike taxis operate illegally with inflated prices (100,000 VND vs older 50,000 VND rates) and lack safety compliance; hiking is strenuous but rewarding
